definition: An article of clothing that is worn on the upper part of the body, and often has sleeves, either long or short, that cover the arms.
example: Employees are required to wear a collared shirt to the office on weekdays.
exampleVi: Nhân viên phải mặc áo sơ mi có cổ khi đến văn phòng vào các ngày trong tuần.
collocations: button-down shirt, iron a shirt, dress shirt

definition: (Manchester) An outer garment that covers the body from the waist downwards, covering each leg separately, usually as far as the ankles; trousers.
example: He spilled coffee on his pants right before the meeting started.
exampleVi: Anh ấy làm đổ cà phê lên quần ngay trước khi cuộc họp bắt đầu.
collocations: a pair of pants, dress pants, wear pants

definition: An article of clothing that covers the part of the body between the waist and the ankles or knees, and is divided into a separate part for each leg.
example: The tailor took his measurements to shorten the trousers.
exampleVi: Người thợ may đo số đo của anh ấy để cắt ngắn quần dài.
collocations: a pair of trousers, tailored trousers, hem trousers

definition: A piece of clothing worn on the upper body outside a shirt or blouse, often waist length to thigh length.
example: She put on a light jacket before heading out to the client meeting.
exampleVi: Cô ấy khoác một chiếc áo khoác mỏng trước khi đi gặp khách hàng.
collocations: wear a jacket, leather jacket, zip up a jacket

definition: An outer garment, typically with sleeves, worn over other clothing for warmth or protection.
example: You should bring a warm coat; the office building can get quite cold in winter.
exampleVi: Bạn nên mang theo áo khoác dày vì tòa nhà văn phòng khá lạnh vào mùa đông.
collocations: wear a coat, winter coat, hang up a coat

definition: An item of clothing (usually worn by a woman or young girl) which both covers the upper part of the body and includes skirts below the waist.
example: Amy and Mary looked very pretty in their dresses.
exampleVi: Amy và Mary trông rất xinh đẹp trong bộ váy của họ.
collocations: wear a dress, formal dress, dress code

definition: An article of clothing, usually worn by women and girls, that hangs from the waist and covers the lower part of the body.
example: The dress code allows employees to wear a skirt with a blazer to work.
exampleVi: Quy định trang phục cho phép nhân viên mặc váy kết hợp với áo blazer khi đi làm.
collocations: wear a skirt, pencil skirt, knee-length skirt

definition: A set of clothes to be worn together, now especially a man's matching jacket and trousers (also business suit or lounge suit), or a similar outfit for a woman.
example: Nick hired a navy-blue suit for the wedding.
exampleVi: Nick đã thuê một bộ đồ màu xanh nước biển cho đám cưới.
collocations: wear a suit, business suit, tailored suit

definition: A long, narrow strip of fabric worn around the neck, typically knotted, as part of formal or business attire.
example: Most managers at the company wear a shirt and tie for client presentations.
exampleVi: Hầu hết các quản lý trong công ty đều mặc sơ mi và thắt cà vạt khi thuyết trình với khách hàng.
collocations: wear a tie, loosen a tie, silk tie

definition: A covering for the head, often in the approximate form of a cone or a cylinder closed at its top end, and sometimes having a brim and other decoration.
example: Construction workers must wear a hard hat at all times on site.
exampleVi: Công nhân xây dựng phải đội mũ bảo hộ mọi lúc tại công trường.
collocations: wear a hat, hard hat, take off a hat

definition: A knitted or woven covering for the foot.
example: He forgot to pack extra socks for his business trip.
exampleVi: Anh ấy quên mang thêm tất cho chuyến công tác.
collocations: a pair of socks, wool socks, wear socks

definition: A protective covering for the foot, with a bottom part composed of thick leather or plastic sole and often a thicker heel, and a softer upper part made of leather or synthetic material. Shoes generally do not extend above the ankle, as opposed to boots, which do.
example: Get your shoes on now, or you'll be late for school.
exampleVi: Hãy mang giày vào ngay nếu không sẽ muộn học.
collocations: wear shoes, dress shoes, a pair of shoes

definition: A heavy shoe that covers part of the leg.
example: Warehouse staff are required to wear steel-toed boots for safety.
exampleVi: Nhân viên kho phải đi ủng mũi thép để đảm bảo an toàn.
collocations: wear boots, safety boots, a pair of boots

definition: An item of clothing other than a mitten, covering all or part of the hand and fingers, but usually allowing independent movement of the fingers.
example: I wore gloves to keep my hands warm.
exampleVi: Tôi đeo găng tay để giữ ấm tay.
collocations: wear gloves, protective gloves, put on gloves

definition: A long, often knitted, garment worn around the neck.
example: She wrapped a scarf around her neck before walking to the office in the cold.
exampleVi: Cô ấy quàng khăn quanh cổ trước khi đi bộ đến văn phòng trong thời tiết lạnh.
collocations: wear a scarf, silk scarf, wrap a scarf

definition: A band worn around the waist to hold clothing to one's body (usually pants), hold weapons (such as a gun or sword), or serve as a decorative piece of clothing.
example: As part of the act, the fat clown's belt broke, causing his pants to fall down.
exampleVi: Khi đang biểu diễn, thắt lưng của chú hề béo bị đứt khiến quần của anh ta tụt xuống.
collocations: wear a belt, leather belt, tighten a belt

definition: A bag stitched to an item of clothing, used for carrying small items.
example: He keeps his employee ID card in his jacket pocket.
exampleVi: Anh ấy để thẻ nhân viên trong túi áo khoác.
collocations: front pocket, jacket pocket, put in a pocket

definition: A small fastener sewn onto clothing and passed through a buttonhole to hold two pieces of fabric together.
example: One of the buttons on his shirt came loose during the presentation.
exampleVi: Một trong những chiếc nút áo sơ mi của anh ấy bị lỏng trong buổi thuyết trình.
collocations: sew on a button, button up, press a button

definition: A zip fastener.
example: The zipper on her bag got stuck right before the flight.
exampleVi: Dây kéo trên túi xách của cô ấy bị kẹt ngay trước chuyến bay.
collocations: zip up, broken zipper, fasten a zipper

definition: Anything that encircles the neck.
example: The manager asked employees to keep their collar buttoned during client visits.
exampleVi: Quản lý yêu cầu nhân viên cài kín cổ áo khi tiếp khách hàng.
collocations: shirt collar, buttoned collar, turn up a collar

definition: A distinctive set of clothes worn by members of an organization, especially at work, that identifies them as belonging to it.
example: All staff at the hotel are required to wear a company uniform.
exampleVi: Tất cả nhân viên khách sạn đều phải mặc đồng phục công ty.
collocations: wear a uniform, company uniform, school uniform

definition: Loose, lightweight clothing worn in bed or for lounging at home.
example: After the late shift, he changed straight into his pyjamas and went to sleep.
exampleVi: Sau ca làm việc muộn, anh ấy thay ngay sang đồ ngủ và đi ngủ.
collocations: wear pyjamas, a pair of pyjamas, silk pyjamas

definition: Clothing worn under other garments, typically next to the skin.
example: The store's new clothing line includes casual wear and underwear.
exampleVi: Bộ sưu tập quần áo mới của cửa hàng bao gồm cả đồ mặc thường ngày và đồ lót.
collocations: wear underwear, thermal underwear, a piece of underwear

definition: A knitted garment worn on the upper body, typically with long sleeves, for warmth.
example: She kept a warm sweater at her desk in case the office air conditioning was too cold.
exampleVi: Cô ấy để sẵn một chiếc áo len ấm ở bàn làm việc phòng khi máy lạnh văn phòng quá lạnh.
collocations: wear a sweater, wool sweater, knit a sweater

definition: A knitted sweater that fastens down the front, typically with buttons.
example: He wore a gray cardigan over his shirt for the office meeting.
exampleVi: Anh ấy khoác một chiếc áo len cardigan màu xám bên ngoài áo sơ mi khi họp.
collocations: wear a cardigan, button up a cardigan, wool cardigan

definition: A loose-fitting upper garment for women, typically buttoned at the front.
example: She bought a new blouse to wear for the client presentation.
exampleVi: Cô ấy mua một chiếc áo cánh mới để mặc trong buổi thuyết trình với khách hàng.
collocations: wear a blouse, silk blouse, sleeveless blouse

definition: Casual trousers made of denim, typically blue.
example: The company allows employees to wear jeans on Fridays.
exampleVi: Công ty cho phép nhân viên mặc quần jean vào thứ Sáu.
collocations: wear jeans, a pair of jeans, ripped jeans

definition: Short trousers that end above or at the knee.
example: The dress code prohibits employees from wearing shorts to the office.
exampleVi: Quy định trang phục cấm nhân viên mặc quần short khi đi làm.
collocations: wear shorts, a pair of shorts, cargo shorts

definition: Casual athletic shoes, typically made of canvas or leather with a rubber sole.
example: Many tech companies now allow employees to wear sneakers in the office.
exampleVi: Nhiều công ty công nghệ hiện cho phép nhân viên đi giày thể thao khi làm việc.
collocations: wear sneakers, a pair of sneakers, white sneakers

definition: Open shoes consisting of a sole held onto the foot by straps.
example: He wore sandals during the company's casual summer event.
exampleVi: Anh ấy đi dép xăng đan trong sự kiện mùa hè trang phục thoải mái của công ty.
collocations: wear sandals, a pair of sandals, leather sandals

definition: Clothing, especially fashionable or specialized clothing sold commercially.
example: The company specializes in manufacturing outdoor apparel for retail stores.
exampleVi: Công ty chuyên sản xuất trang phục ngoài trời để bán cho các cửa hàng bán lẻ.
collocations: sports apparel, apparel industry, apparel brand

definition: A fine, soft fabric produced by silkworms, often used for luxury clothing.
example: The designer used silk to create the new evening dress collection.
exampleVi: Nhà thiết kế đã sử dụng lụa để tạo ra bộ sưu tập váy dạ hội mới.
collocations: silk fabric, silk tie, pure silk

definition: Clothing, especially of a particular or formal kind.
example: Business attire is required for all employees attending the conference.
exampleVi: Trang phục công sở là bắt buộc đối với tất cả nhân viên tham dự hội nghị.
collocations: business attire, formal attire, casual attire

definition: A set of clothes worn to represent a particular character, style, or period, often for a performance or event.
example: The staff wore matching costumes for the company's promotional event.
exampleVi: Nhân viên mặc trang phục đồng bộ cho sự kiện quảng bá của công ty.
collocations: wear a costume, costume design, traditional costume

definition: A person whose job is to make, repair, or alter clothes, especially suits.
example: He took his suit to the tailor to have the sleeves shortened.
exampleVi: Anh ấy mang bộ vest đến thợ may để chỉnh ngắn tay áo.
collocations: visit a tailor, tailor-made, hire a tailor

definition: A strong, lightweight fabric made from flax fibers, often used for summer clothing.
example: The store offers a range of linen shirts suitable for warm climates.
exampleVi: Cửa hàng cung cấp nhiều loại áo sơ mi vải lanh phù hợp với khí hậu nóng.
collocations: linen shirt, linen fabric, pure linen

definition: A single item of clothing.
example: Each garment is inspected for quality before it leaves the factory.
exampleVi: Mỗi sản phẩm may mặc đều được kiểm tra chất lượng trước khi xuất xưởng.
collocations: garment industry, quality garment, garment factory

definition: Material made from animal hide, often used for shoes, belts, and jackets.
example: The company imports leather from several suppliers to make its handbags.
exampleVi: Công ty nhập khẩu da từ nhiều nhà cung cấp để sản xuất túi xách.
collocations: leather jacket, genuine leather, leather goods

definition: A warm fabric made from the hair of sheep or other animals.
example: The winter collection features coats made from pure wool.
exampleVi: Bộ sưu tập mùa đông có các áo khoác làm từ len nguyên chất.
collocations: wool sweater, pure wool, wool fabric

definition: A soft, breathable fabric made from the fibers of the cotton plant.
example: The shirts are made of 100% cotton for comfort in hot weather.
exampleVi: Những chiếc áo sơ mi được làm từ 100% cotton để tạo cảm giác thoải mái trong thời tiết nóng.
collocations: cotton shirt, pure cotton, cotton fabric

definition: A set of clothes worn together for a particular occasion.
example: She chose a professional outfit for her first day at the new job.
exampleVi: Cô ấy chọn một bộ trang phục chuyên nghiệp cho ngày đầu tiên đi làm ở công ty mới.
collocations: put together an outfit, matching outfit, business outfit

definition: Cloth material produced by weaving or knitting fibers.
example: The designer selected a lightweight fabric for the summer collection.
exampleVi: Nhà thiết kế đã chọn loại vải nhẹ cho bộ sưu tập mùa hè.
collocations: choose a fabric, durable fabric, fabric sample

definition: A synthetic fabric known for being durable, wrinkle-resistant, and inexpensive.
example: Many uniforms are made from a blend of cotton and polyester.
exampleVi: Nhiều bộ đồng phục được làm từ hỗn hợp cotton và polyester.
collocations: polyester blend, polyester fabric, made of polyester

definition: A type of cloth or woven fabric, often used to refer to the industry that produces it.
example: The company operates several textile factories across the region.
exampleVi: Công ty vận hành nhiều nhà máy dệt trong khu vực.
collocations: textile industry, textile factory, textile production

definition: The fabric or substance from which clothing or products are made.
example: The supplier provides high-quality material for the new clothing line.
exampleVi: Nhà cung cấp cung cấp nguyên liệu chất lượng cao cho dòng sản phẩm quần áo mới.
collocations: raw material, quality material, choose material

definition: A collection of clothes owned by a person, or a piece of furniture for storing clothes.
example: She updated her professional wardrobe before starting the new job.
exampleVi: Cô ấy làm mới tủ đồ công sở trước khi bắt đầu công việc mới.
collocations: update a wardrobe, professional wardrobe, wardrobe malfunction

definition: A session in which clothing is tried on and adjusted to ensure it fits properly.
example: The tailor scheduled a fitting to make sure the suit fit perfectly.
exampleVi: Người thợ may sắp xếp một buổi thử đồ để đảm bảo bộ vest vừa vặn hoàn hảo.
collocations: schedule a fitting, final fitting, fitting room

definition: A woman who sews clothes professionally, especially as an occupation.
example: The seamstress altered the dress to fit the bride perfectly.
exampleVi: Người thợ may nữ đã chỉnh sửa chiếc váy để vừa vặn hoàn hảo với cô dâu.
collocations: hire a seamstress, skilled seamstress, work as a seamstress

definition: A change made to a piece of clothing to adjust its fit or style.
example: The store offers free alterations on all purchased suits.
exampleVi: Cửa hàng cung cấp dịch vụ chỉnh sửa miễn phí cho tất cả các bộ vest đã mua.
collocations: make an alteration, minor alteration, alteration service

definition: A small room or cabinet used for storing clothes and other items.
example: He hung his suits neatly in the office closet.
exampleVi: Anh ấy treo những bộ vest gọn gàng trong tủ quần áo ở văn phòng.
collocations: walk-in closet, hang in a closet, organize a closet

definition: A device, usually shaped like a person's shoulders, used to hang clothes.
example: She hung the new shirts on hangers before placing them on display.
exampleVi: Cô ấy treo những chiếc áo sơ mi mới lên móc trước khi trưng bày.
collocations: wire hanger, hang on a hanger, clothes hanger

definition: A person who creates the appearance and style of clothing or products.
example: The fashion designer unveiled her new collection at the trade show.
exampleVi: Nhà thiết kế thời trang đã ra mắt bộ sưu tập mới tại hội chợ thương mại.
collocations: fashion designer, designer brand, designer clothes

definition: An item, such as a bag, belt, or jewelry, worn to complement an outfit.
example: The store also sells accessories like belts and scarves to match its clothing.
exampleVi: Cửa hàng cũng bán các phụ kiện như thắt lưng và khăn quàng để phối cùng quần áo.
collocations: fashion accessory, matching accessory, accessory line

definition: A category of products manufactured under a particular name, often associated with a specific style or reputation.
example: The clothing brand launched a new line of business attire this season.
exampleVi: Thương hiệu thời trang đã ra mắt một dòng trang phục công sở mới trong mùa này.
collocations: clothing brand, brand name, launch a brand

definition: Shoes, boots, and other items worn to cover the feet.
example: The company expanded its product line to include footwear as well as clothing.
exampleVi: Công ty đã mở rộng dòng sản phẩm để bao gồm cả giày dép ngoài quần áo.
collocations: footwear industry, comfortable footwear, footwear brand

definition: A small piece of fabric or paper attached to clothing showing information such as size, material, or brand.
example: Always check the care label before washing a new garment.
exampleVi: Luôn kiểm tra nhãn hướng dẫn giặt trước khi giặt một sản phẩm may mặc mới.
collocations: clothing label, care label, designer label

definition: An open-topped box that can be slid in and out of the cabinet that contains it, used for storing clothing or other articles.
example: She keeps her folded shirts in the top drawer of the dresser.
exampleVi: Cô ấy để những chiếc áo sơ mi gấp gọn trong ngăn kéo trên cùng của tủ.
collocations: open a drawer, sock drawer, chest of drawers

definition: Clothing for non-formal occasions.
example: The office relaxed its dress code to allow casualwear on Fridays.
exampleVi: Văn phòng đã nới lỏng quy định trang phục để cho phép mặc đồ thường ngày vào thứ Sáu.
collocations: casualwear line, comfortable casualwear, casualwear brand

definition: Clothing worn for formal occasions, such as suits and evening gowns.
example: Guests were asked to wear formalwear to the company's annual gala.
exampleVi: Khách mời được yêu cầu mặc trang phục lịch sự đến buổi tiệc thường niên của công ty.
collocations: wear formalwear, rent formalwear, formalwear shop

definition: Casual clothing that is comfortable to wear when exercising or partaking in sport; activewear.
example: The company launched a new line of sportswear designed for gym use.
exampleVi: Công ty đã ra mắt một dòng trang phục thể thao mới dành cho tập gym.
collocations: sportswear brand, comfortable sportswear, sportswear line
